# B018. Bayesian Black-Litterman with ML Views

## Positioning

This is the most natural place to blend machine-learned views into a portfolio prior without pretending the ML score is certainty. The Bayesian layer should dominate when the view is weak and relax only when the evidence is stable.

## Reference Basis

- [View fusion vis-à-vis a Bayesian interpretation of Black-Litterman for portfolio allocation](../../references/papers/2301.13594_black_litterman_fusion.pdf) - posterior view fusion for the core allocation step
- [LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree](../../references/papers/lightgbm_neurips_2017.pdf) - ML view generator and tabular baseline

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Fundamental Stack

Point-in-time fundamentals and macro-aware feature joins for slower rebalance cycles.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - fundamental and macro data loader
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature joins and lag alignment
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - tabular score model for the hybrid stack

### Cross-Sectional Ranker

The executable ranking path that turns scores into a tradable long/flat or top-k book.
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - LightGBM scoring model
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature matrix construction for the ranker

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use cross-sectional factor data, technicals, and any macro or fundamental views that can be aligned point-in-time.
- Build the ML view on the same feature matrix used by the existing LightGBM path.
- Keep the posterior inputs explicit so the allocation can be audited later.

## Execution Path

- Generate a set of ML views and fuse them with a Bayesian prior into portfolio weights.
- Clip overconfident views and cap the influence of highly correlated signals.
- Pass the resulting weights through the same risk and execution gates as every other candidate.

## Risk Controls

- Reject any ML view that is not backed by point-in-time data.
- Limit the influence of correlated views so the posterior does not overfit one factor cluster.
- Do not let the allocator become more volatile than the baseline it is supposed to improve.

## Validation Gates

- Compare the posterior portfolio to F002 and F012 on both return and concentration metrics.
- Track how often the posterior changes weights compared with the ML-only view.
- Measure whether the Bayesian fusion improves stability without sacrificing too much upside.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the ML views overpower the prior and turn the model into a glorified ranker.
- Archive if turnover or concentration becomes worse than the baseline.
- Archive if the posterior cannot be interpreted after a few rebalance cycles.

## Next Step

Use this as the canonical example of a Bayesian overlay on top of an ML ranker.
